svg.text: Service Decomposition Strategies
Four approaches to decomposing a monolith: by business capability, subdomain, verb, and data ownership
By Business Capability
Decompose based on organizational structure and business functions
Services: User Mgmt, Order Processing, Inventory, Payment, Shipping
✅ Aligns with team structure, intuitive boundaries
⚠ May create coarse-grained services with internal coupling
By Subdomain (Domain-Driven Design)
Decompose based on bounded contexts and ubiquitous language
Services: Core domain (Order), Supporting (User), Generic (Payment)
✅ Strong domain boundaries, clear data ownership
⚠ Requires DDD expertise, time-consuming analysis
By Verb (Action)
Decompose based on operations: Query Service, Command Service
Services: Read Service (queries), Write Service (mutations)
✅ Natural CQRS split, independent read/write scaling
⚠ Cross-cutting concerns split, complex data consistency
By Data Ownership
Decompose based on data tables/entities and their relationships
Services: User Data Service, Order Data Service, Product Data Service
✅ Clear data boundaries, easy database-per-service
⚠ May create anemic services (CRUD-only, no business logic)
Recommended Approach: Hybrid (DDD + Business Capability)
1. Identify bounded contexts using DDD (subdomain analysis)
2. Align services with business capabilities (team structure, organizational boundaries)
3. Ensure each service owns its data (database-per-service)
4. Use events for cross-service communication (not shared databases or synchronous calls)
This hybrid approach provides strong domain boundaries while aligning with the organization's
structure, ensuring that each service can be developed, deployed, and operated independently.
✅ Independent deployability | ✅ Clear data ownership | ✅ Team autonomy | ✅ Fault isolation
